description: Canonical local Cloudflare architecture rules for DevGov-governed loopback services. Host-local config paths and credentials stay in reports/.

items.requirement: Every Cloudflare-exposed local service must have a governed loopback origin in the port registry before public route promotion.
items.verification: Run port preflight and service health checks before public probing.
items.notes: Default origin host is 127.0.0.1 unless a reviewed exception documents wider exposure.

items.requirement: Each hostname must be represented by a public route record with exposure class, Access requirement, local target, and health URL.
items.verification: Run public route scans and validate duplicate hostnames before promotion.
items.notes: Public health routes must expose minimal readiness only and must not execute service commands.

items.requirement: Protected app or API routes should require Cloudflare Access unless intentionally classified as public health.
items.verification: Confirm Access requirement and route behavior together before marking routes approved.
items.notes: A route without Access requires explicit exposure review.

items.requirement: Tunnel startup and restart paths must be represented by startup governance records and remain review-gated.
items.verification: Confirm backup or rollback evidence before applying tunnel startup changes.
items.notes: Dashboard execution stays disabled until command boundaries are reviewed.

items.requirement: Credential files, tunnel config paths, certs, private keys, API tokens, process IDs, and logs must not enter canonical registries.
items.verification: Registry validators reject machine-local paths and credential-looking values.
items.notes: Keep host-local discovery evidence in generated reports only.
